_comment: Opus-authored, keyless world envelope for the playable quest loop (SPEC 2026-07-01). The Clockmaker's Loft. Load with `bin/game world load worlds/clockmakers-loft.json` (or `bin/game world reset --yes`). No LLM call, no API key. The quest: read the repair-ledger -> take the escapement gear by the well -> give it to Tace in the loft (earns the case-key) -> use the case-key on the clock-case -> open the case (the great clock ticks again). All quest beats are pre-baked authored strings; the local LLM only voices ambient talk/drift. Cross-references (Tace.wants, clock-case use.with) are BY NAME. Internal world_id stays w-bunny (loader hardcodes it).

world.name: The Clockmaker's Loft
world.aesthetic_seed: a small clockmaker's village at dusk, warm lantern-light on brass and old oak, watercolor edges, the gentle mystery of a great clock stopped and waiting to be mended

rooms.title: The Stopped Clock
rooms.seed: the base of a great stopped clock, its brass face gone still and patient, dust turning slow in a shaft of late gold; a tall glass-fronted case stands against one wall, and a worn ledger waits open on a lectern

rooms.title: The Clockmaker's Loft
rooms.seed: a clockmaker's loft under the eaves, warm with the smell of oil and brass, shelves of small resting clocks ticking softly out of time with one another, a round window full of amber dusk

rooms.title: The Lantern Square
rooms.seed: a small village square at dusk, paper lanterns coming up one by one in warm pools of light, moths turning slow above the cobbles, the stones still holding the last of the day's heat

rooms.title: The Old Workshop
rooms.seed: a dim old workshop just off the square, curls of brass and cedar shavings soft underfoot, a broom leaned patient in the corner, one long blade of slant light through a shutter

rooms.title: The Mossy Well-Court
rooms.seed: a quiet well-court at the edge of the village, the old stone well cool and green with moss, ferns nodding at its foot, a hush broken now and then by a far, slow drip of water

toons.name: Wick
toons.seed: a traveler just come to the village, curious and easy-going, fond of small mended things
toons.appearance_seed: a soft watercolor figure in a travel-worn coat the color of dusk, a satchel worn crosswise, an open and unhurried face

toons.name: Tace
toons.seed: the village clockmaker, patient and precise, gone a little quiet and sad in the season since the great clock stopped; warm underneath, and slow to hope
toons.appearance_seed: a slight person in a leather apron hung with fine brass tools, spectacles pushed up into greying hair, ink and oil worn into careful hands
toons.presence_text: Tace looks up from a bench of stopped clocks, sets down a fine brass tool, and offers a tired, kind nod.

gives.name: case key
gives.seed: a small brass case-key on a loop of worn ribbon, warm as if just out of a pocket

properties.gives_text: Tace turns the little gear over in the lamplight, and something long held eases out of their shoulders. 'You found it. You carried it all the way home.' They fold a small brass key into your hand, warm from their pocket. 'The case is yours to open now, friend.'
properties.declines_text: Tace considers what you're holding out, and smiles a small, tired smile. 'That's kind of you. But it isn't the little thing the clock is missing.'

toons.name: Bell
toons.seed: the one who lights the square's lanterns at dusk; bright, kind, and talkative in short warm bursts; notices small things
toons.appearance_seed: a wiry young person with a lamplighter's hooked pole, soot smudged along one cheek, a scarf the color of butter-yellow lamplight
toons.presence_text: Bell is up a small ladder coaxing a lantern alight, and lifts a sooty hand in greeting.

toons.name: Mott
toons.seed: the one who sweeps the old workshop; quiet, unhurried, and fond of small found things kept in a tin
toons.appearance_seed: a broad, calm person in a dust-grey smock, sleeves rolled, a soft broom worn to a comfortable angle, a tin of oddments in one pocket
toons.presence_text: Mott pauses mid-sweep, leans on the broom, and gives you an unhurried, friendly look.

items.name: repair ledger
items.seed: a worn leather ledger open on a lectern, its soft pages close-written in a fading, careful hand
items.text: The great clock has stood still since the first hard frost. The fault is the escapement gear -- the small brass heart of the whole mechanism -- which slipped its pin one cold night and rolled away down the lane, south, toward the old well at the edge of the village. Whoever carries it back to Tace in the loft will have earned the case-key, which hangs, by long and gentle custom, with whoever mends the clock.

items.name: clock case
items.seed: a tall glass-fronted clock case of dark oak, its little brass door held by a delicate lock

state_text.locked: A small brass lock holds the little door fast.
state_text.unlocked: The lock hangs sprung now, though the door is still gently closed.
state_text.open: The door stands open, and behind the glass the pendulum sways bright and sure.

properties.locked_text: The clock case is locked; its small brass lock won't give without the right key.
properties.open_text: The little lock gives, the case door swings wide, and behind the glass the pendulum stirs, swings, and catches the light -- and high overhead the great clock takes its first slow tick in a long, long while.

use.text: The case-key turns with a soft, satisfying click, and the little brass lock springs open under your thumb.

contains.name: warm brass cog
contains.seed: a small spare brass cog, warm as if just held, its fine teeth catching the last of the light

items.name: escapement gear
items.seed: a small brass gear resting in the moss at the well's foot, its fine teeth flecked with green, cool from the shadow but sound and whole

items.name: brass pendulum
items.seed: a spare brass pendulum hung from a nail, turning slow and catching the amber window-light

items.name: paper lantern
items.seed: a paper lantern on a hooked pole, its small flame warm and steady against the deepening blue

skills.name: wind
skills.description: Wind one of the loft's small resting clocks.
skills.prompt_template:
You wind one of the clockmaker's small resting clocks in the loft. Your gesture: {{ player_input }}
Reply with ONE soft, painterly `narrate` effect (cozy, Spiritfarer-warm, a tick starting up soft and uncertain like it is remembering how, brass and amber dusk, no urgency, no modern tech). Return JSON with an 'effects' list.

skills.name: listen
skills.description: Listen at the mouth of the old well.
skills.prompt_template:
You lean and listen at the mouth of the old mossy well. Your gesture: {{ player_input }}
Reply with ONE soft, painterly `narrate` effect (cozy, Spiritfarer-warm, cool stone and moss and a far slow drip echoing up, a hush, no urgency, no modern tech). Return JSON with an 'effects' list.
